name: Make support.hub deep links select Support-hub scope before navigation
description: The support.hub deep link only pushes the Mentor route; from a supporter's Me scope that route still renders the learner Mentor surface because active scope is unchanged.
acceptanceCriteria:
- Activating a support.hub pointer first selects the Support-hub scope and then opens its Mentor tab.
- The transition works from Me and every person scope and remains safe if the edge list changed.
- Back navigation has a defined scope behavior and does not duplicate support content into Me.
- Route-contract and seeded multi-scope tests cover the pointer.
bodyText:
Acceptance Criteria (PM-ratified 2026-07-19, Opus-drafted, grounded at main 85629757c)
Risk/Impact: P2 wrong-surface bug — the support.hub pointer only pushes /(app)/mentor; from Me/person scope the active scope is unchanged, so the learner Mentor surface renders instead of the supporter hub.
AC-1 (scope-before-nav — the guaranteed property): Activating a support.hub pointer first calls setActiveScope({kind:'supporter-hub'}) (scope-context.tsx:125-145) and only then opens the Mentor tab (/(app)/mentor, now-deep-link.ts:58), so the supporter-hub Mentor surface renders. Evidence: co-located jest with a spy ordering assertion at each caller — apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/mentor.tsx and apps/mobile/src/components/journal/JournalMomentsStrip.tsx (the two pushNowDeepLink sites).
AC-2 (every source scope + stale-edge safety): The transition produces the supporter-hub surface from Me scope and from every person scope, and is safe (no throw, defined fallback) if the supportership edge list changed since the pointer was minted (setActiveScope no-ops for unknown non-me scopes — scope-context.tsx:136). Evidence: seeded multi-scope cases in the caller/now-deep-link co-located test.
AC-3 (Back scope behavior): Back navigation from the support-hub Mentor surface has defined scope behavior and does not duplicate support content into the Me scope. Evidence: co-located jest on the return path; any visible layout claim via a named full nav-shell.spec.ts case.
AC-4 (flag/shape isolation): A learner-shape / me-only account is unaffected (setActiveScope early-returns) and legacy/V0 non-supporter flows are unchanged. Evidence: named case in the co-located suite.
Refinement notes: Effort=M. Core issue: pushNowDeepLink takes only Pick<Router,'push'> (now-deep-link.ts:92) — no scope access; thread setActiveScope via a new option or via the two callers which already hold useScopeContext. Target is a tab root: the property is scope-selected-before-navigation, NOT ancestor-chain depth — no reflex chain AC.
